content_gaps: There is very little content comparing different cultural dragons (Chinese vs. Japanese vs. Celtic vs. Viking) side-by-side within the same article; most pages focus on just one.

The distinction between malevolent Western dragons and benevolent Eastern dragons is often mentioned but not deeply explored, missing nuances in European folklore where dragons were also symbols of sovereignty and power (e.g., Pendragon).
Specific dragon postures and forms in jewelry (e.g., ouroboros, coiled dragons, dragons holding pearls) are not well-explained. The meaning of the pearl is a significant omission.
The connection between the material (gold, silver, jade) and the dragon's meaning is only superficially touched upon by a few sites.
There is a lack of content connecting dragon symbolism to specific occasions for gifting the jewelry (e.g., graduation, new business, etc.).
